codeReview.summary: Fail. The new sandbox identity map only distinguishes duplicate-named sandbox agents by a structural signature, so two same-name sandbox agents with identical definitions collapse onto one key/runtime. That means handoffs/resume can reuse the same workspace for distinct agent instances, which violates the feature requirement that duplicate-named sandbox agents preserve distinct isolated identities across JSON round-trips.

issues.description: `collect_reachable_agents()` and `build_sandbox_identity_map()` key sandbox identities by `(agent.name, sandbox_agent_signature)` instead of per-agent instance identity. For duplicate-named sandbox agents whose model/instructions/manifest/handoff-target names are the same, `collect_reachable_agents()` drops the later instance as already visited (lines 875-885), and `key_by_signature.insert(...)` overwrites the earlier mapping for the same signature (line 940). Both agent instances then resolve to the same sandbox key/runtime, so their workspace state can leak and resume cannot preserve distinct identities. The added regression test only covers duplicate names with different instructions/manifests/models (`crates/openai-agents/tests/sandbox_semantics.rs:2160-2172`), so this collision case is still untested.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared sandbox guidance does not call out an adversarial case for duplicate-named sandbox agents that are otherwise structurally identical, and the feature tests only exercised duplicate names with different manifests/instructions/models. Adding that case to the sandbox worker guidance or validation coverage would make this class of identity-collision bug harder to miss.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: rust-sandbox-worker SKILL.md step 3 says to write failing tests first, but there is no duplicate-name identity-collision example; the only new duplicate-name test is `crates/openai-agents/tests/sandbox_semantics.rs:2148-2334`, where the two `sandbox` agents intentionally differ at lines 2160-2172.

summary: Reviewed mission metadata, the worker handoff/transcript skeleton, skill guidance, architecture/services docs, commit `4624a16c42192fe0d01c497f604e9caba0257eee`, and the sandbox semantics tests. The handoff and agent-as-tool paths look aligned with the feature contract, but the duplicate-name identity implementation is still incorrect for structurally identical sandbox agents, so the feature does not fully satisfy the required isolated resume identity behavior.
